title_guess: France: Picardy & Burgundy (St Quentin, Montbard, Dijon, Provins)

pages.text:
Kler + Nana gave us supper in the Swan Hotel. Very good. Conrad has been behaving very well.
	Sunday we packed up + left to watch at the Glad Mountain Gliding Club. We saw about 3 planes take off, [?spun] Fantastic vviews + [?ridge]. ([?we forgot [illegible]to])
	On for fish + chips in Abergavenny. On to Raglan Castle. Lovely sunshine.
	Then on to home.
__Tuesday [?7/6]th August__
	10 we left home + dropped Tinder off at kennels. The trafic + cars in the waiting area at the Channel Tunnel were lighter than usual.
__Wednesday 7th August__
	We camped next to a lake in a residential camp site, without paying at [?Humancourt] ** between [?Arras] + Cambrai.
	We walked in the woods in the evening.
	The lake was stipled with rain while we had breakfast + it hasn't stopped

raining during our trip on to St. Quentin.
	We visited the wrong big church first + then found the Basilica. Its onomous + vertical.

[Photo: labyrinth floor mosaic]

__Labyrinthe at St Quentin__

__Picardie__
__Catthedrals__

[Small map showing: Amiens, Beauvais, Noyon, Senlis, Laon, St Quentin, Soissons]

After leaving St. Quentin, Simon found a headlight was not working. We drove on to la Barbuise Campsite [illegible] (where we had stayed many years before) & had a walk over chalk fields! Near Troyes.
Thursday
  Simon can't mend the headlight because he's got a different one in the stores. In the end he discovered it was a broken fuse. We travelled south, stopping at an old church on the way which was closed. At Troyes we went to an Auchan shops, OK but a bit down market. We bought a la Reine pizza for lunch which was good, then carried on to Les Treilles Campsite [illegible] Montbard. Charles & Jenny arrived at 4 pm, so we had more tea & put up the tent. Then we went on a bike ride into Montbard. Eve bought athletes foot cream & Charles chose grey (wool) cotton for Eve to knit him a scarf. It's a good campsite - town & shops, restaurant & bike trail (Canal de Bourgogne) & free swimming.
Friday We went on a [illegible] 15 mile bike ride

La forge
de
Buffon

along the Canal & went to Buffon to see the Forge, about 1760, started by Buffon when he was 60. He spent his early life as a Naturalist, categorising birds. Then he built an amazing Forge

with beautiful architecture. Water-wheels powered 3 forges, tilt hammer, washing minerals, bellows. Part of the area is privately owned. We had bread & salami. Then we cycled to Aray+ the only food was delicious patisserie (prunes) We cycled on to Nuits sous Ravières, where we waited 30 min for a train back to Montbard. 17 miles
  Then we swam (too cold) Jenny & Eve also had to rig up the internet on their mobs (free, but we first had to download a film about Pampers.) Tricky. Simon & Eve had trouble with the lockers at the pool. Eve stood shivering because she cdn't work the codes without her glasses which were in the locker. (The following day I cdn't open the washing machine to get the

clothes out.)
  After a rest we went out to a glorious supper. Simon & Jenny had swordfish, avocado & grapefruit. We all had pork.
~~Friday~~ Saturday
  We packed up & drove to Abbey de Fontenay.

[photo: Galerie nord du cloître]
[photo: Notre-Dame de Fontenay (XIIIème siècle)]

It's one of the best places I've seen. Now lived in by the Montgolfier (Balloon descendants) family. The tilt hammer was
[photo: Vue aérienne]
invented he[re] in 1190 or 129[0]

On to Intermarché (actually we went back to ou[r

We arrived at __Ste Marie sur l'Ouche__ &
hung the washing & it dried. The weather is
now warmer & it's not threatening rain.
~~Saturday~~ Sunday
We cycled 27 miles, along the Canal de
Bourgogne away from Dijon to Eclus
Pont de Pany. We had lunch at Bryony's.
An amazing set-up next to the canal by
an English woman who spoke fluent French.
The food was just what we needed, but as
Charles said - English & not good. Simon had
ham & chips, Eve fish fingers & chips, Jenny
chicken nuggets & chips & Charles quiche &
chips - Not a tomato or green veg in sight!
The coffee was good. Jenny had forgotten her
sheepskin saddle cover & had a sore bum.
Eve had a sore bum even with the cover.
Limericks at Supper.

Monday
We set off before Jenny & Charles. They maybe
going to visit Paia. We went towards Sens
for a supermarket & the on to Provins.

We cycled past lots of Ecluses, lock-keepers houses 40[illegible]

[Photo: postcard glued in, captioned "En Bourgogne" — showing a lock-keeper's house beside a canal with mist on the water]

We reached an unusual campsite, a bit like
Center Parks, around gravel pits, Hermes Loisirs
also 'La Fontaine' [illegible] at Gouaix.
There's a bar & one of the pits is a
swimming pool. After tea we rode our
bikes around the site & had a drink at the [bar?]

It's very peaceful + green + pretty with the water. There's a remote control boat. I have shut the van doors so that Simon doesn't fall out when he falls asleep over his book.

Tuesday Simon woke me very early to roll over + look at the mist on the lake. It was very beautiful.
We drove on to Provins. It's only an hour's drive from Paris + has lots of ladies with lots of make-up. It's an old Mediaeval town, very pretty. We didn't see either of the sites (there are 4 main ones) but walked up the hill to Caesar's Tower (taken by the English in the 100 Year War who built a wall around it.).

It's octagon on a square base. We had a very good lunch – pizza + goat's

cheese + honey + crème brulee.

We continued north thru Reims to Bourget Commin. The campsite has a small indoor swimming pool + is friendly. There's a noisy family nearby with a child who doesn't stop crying wolf + screaming like a peacock. We read alot. __La Poste__

Wednesday
I got out of bed + into the pool + washed my hair. After breakfast we cycled. Simon has downloaded Russian military maps off the internet. These Russian maps have contours which are useful for planning routes that avoid steep hills. With the Russian maps there is another layer showing the Open Street Map which is a free world map generated by volunteers and this is good for footpaths and cycling routes. [?F]
We set off, shopping at Carrefour, much more cheerful than Auchan. We had lunch on the carpark. We went off on cycle ride along by fields + woods + canal, trying to stay level. There was an Italian [strikethrough: cemeti] cemetery + a British one. It was a [?sunger] ride. We got back for lunch. Then we read. We had

Supper in the restaurant of the camp-site.
There were nice people on the pitch near us — Eena & Christopher. He is organist at Freibourg. They have a smart, modern, yellow caravan.

Thursday
We set off & stopped at Carrefour, more cheapful than Auchan, lunch on the carpark. At about 2 pm we stopped at Abbeville, outside the old address of au pair Florence. Her mother invited us in. The family don't speak English. Florence now lives in Calais, but is on holiday in Corsica. Florence's mother is Genevieve. Her friend was there (a man) & grandmother & 2 nieces, Lucinda (15) & a 6 year old. Lucinda wants to visit England. Her mother died 3 years ago. The 6 year old is fun. The grandmother's first child died aged 1 in a bombardment. The second died of cancer 3 years ago. Very sad. The girls live in

Perpignan & are on holiday at their grandmother's.
Genevieve chatted to Florence on the phone, gave us a list of restaurants at Le Crotoy & booked us into her cousin's campsite at Novelles.
We reached the campsite & found a Chinese WW I Cemetry right next [to] it, so I took photos for Mark O'Neil.
__Aire Haies de Novelles__ *** very clean loos but unisex. :" Sheep & a donkey nearby but quiet campers. So odd that the Chinese Labour Corps were treated as prisoners, but have a magnificent Lutyens graveyard, [it] just like the Brits.

Friday
We went to get a map at the station, cycled to Le Crotoy. Not a very nice [bike] ride, next to a road. The tide was out all the time we were there (11.45–3) & it was interesting to watch people on the sand/mud in the bay.
We ate at Modo Modo. Very good food but

slow service. The first course looked like chipboard. __Mussels were__ Simon had mussels + I had fish soup. We had a nice selection of fish + lovely puttings - nougat ice cream + raspberry coulis.
     The village was over-packed with people in the market, but on our way back, we found it had all been cleared away.
     We had intended to take the little train home at 4, but as we left the restaurant it spotted with rain + we decided to cycle. The wind was with us. We left at 2.50 + got back to the van at 3.50.
     We set off towards Calais.
     The first campsite we found at Bien Assisse in Guines was full. They sent us on to __La Petite Source__ *** It was crowded + the people were quite rough but friendly. Simon went on a bike ride + Eve read. The drive to the campsite was

very bumpy. The Marais (bog) was around us. Lots of fishermen.
     A red fire engine arrived with lights flashing + we crowded around with the children to see the fire. But a nice man came up + explained that the red vans are in fact ambulances + it had been called - he mimed kicking - for domestic violence, so we were embarrassed to have been watching.
__Saturday__ August 17th 2013
     We packed up + switched off the gas + left with no rush. It took only 12 mins to the booking-in place + we caught a train 2 before our booked one. We motored up to Bucks. We lunched + walked down a path onto a common.
White Mark Farm   (no. 82)
Watlington (near care home) we looked at this good campsite for staying with William + Joseph in the autumn. Nr. Chvdan [?] Arms Pub.
